actions.proposedValue:
Scene function: Mara's hearing strips more than status: Ashren publicly revokes her charter, names her last expedition a fraud, and makes further mapping illegal without Survey permission.
Goal: Mara tries to defend the expedition report well enough to keep legal standing.
Conflict: Ashren controls the hearing record and twists every map discrepancy into evidence of incompetence.
Turn/value shift: Mara notices Ashren already knows the missing province should exist, so the humiliation becomes proof of a cover-up.
Outcome: Mara loses the charter, public credibility, and lawful access to Survey archives.
Consequence: Any attempt to keep investigating now risks arrest and permanent erasure from the cartographers' rolls.
actions.operatorNote: Make the cost and next-conflict pressure explicit before drafting.
actions.rationale: The scene already has the right event; the plan needs sharper irreversible cost and downstream pressure.

actions.operatorNote: Single-character discovery scene; relationship movement is not the target.
Ashren is materially active because he revokes the charter and controls the hearing outcome.
The dock negotiation has enough plan-level motivation; watch for prose execution.

details.note: Diagnostic clear observation for explicit disposition-plan plumbing; not a human quality label.
